div1.entryFree:
Fat, vb. 1) trans. a) to fatten: Mids. II, 1, 97. Hml. II, 2, 607. IV, 3, 23. IV, 3, 23
b) to nourish: manhood and honour should have hare-hearts, would they but f. their thoughts with this crammed reason, Troil. II, 2, 48. Figuratively, to refresh, to delight: how this villany doth f. me with the very thoughts of it, Tit. III, 1, 204.
2) intr. to grow fat: he is franked up to --ing for his pains, R3 I, 3, 314 (perhaps here also trans.).
